title: The Sieve — JS DOM inventory function
description: resources/sieve.js — deterministic JavaScript function for structured DOM inventory. sieve/core.clj — inject JS via bridge execute-script, parse results into Clojure data. sieve/taxonomy.clj — element classification (interactable/output/structural/image), region path derivation from landmarks and ARIA. Verify: from REPL, run sieve on a live page, get structured inventory matching the element record spec from ARCHITECTURE.md.
design: Files: resources/sieve.js (the JS function), leftglove/sieve/core.clj (orchestration — inject + parse), leftglove/sieve/taxonomy.clj (classification rules, region derivation). Consumes: bridge/browser execute-script. Produces: structured page inventory as Clojure data.
acceptance_criteria: From REPL: (sieve/run! browser) returns a map matching the page inventory spec from ARCHITECTURE.md. Elements have category, tag, label, locators, state, rect, region. Tested against at least two different sites.

title: SVOI vocabulary projection as MCP tools
description: Read SL glossaries (subjects, verbs), interfaces, and intent regions via bridge. Project vocabulary as dynamic MCP tool schemas — agent sees available subjects, verbs per interface, objects per IR per interface. SVOI dispatch: compose step text from tuple components, execute via SL REPL functions (step/as). Auto-observe after every action. Action edge persistence to graph. Verify: agent sees SVOI vocabulary as tools, can execute an action, gets post-action observation, action edge in graph.
design: Files: leftglove/tools/svoi.clj (vocabulary reading, schema projection, dispatch), leftglove/mcp/schema.clj (SVOI-to-MCP-tool-schema transform). Consumes: bridge (glossaries, step registry, IRs), sieve (auto-observe), graph/writes (action edges). Produces: dynamic MCP tools from SVOI state.
acceptance_criteria: Agent sees dynamically generated tools reflecting loaded SVOI vocabulary. Agent executes a verb (e.g. click) on an object, gets post-action observation. Action edge exists in graph linking before/after observations.
notes: STALE: Rescope for demo. Demo needs: read glossary files → project as MCP tool schemas. Full SVOI dispatch + auto-observe + graph edges = post-demo.

Verify: agent can query observations from graph, agent can annotate an element, annotation persists.

title: Dogfood: Gherkin scenarios — SL tests the TL UI
description:
Write Gherkin scenarios where ShiftLefter drives the toddler loop UI as the app under test. All scenarios use :test as the subject (mechanical verification, not behavioral specs).

Scenarios to cover:
- Navigate: :test enters URL in ToddlerLoop.url-input, clicks navigate, screenshot appears
- Sieve: :test clicks sieve-btn, elements inventory populates, status shows element count
- Classify: :test presses keyboard shortcuts (c/t/r/x/u), classified-count increments, overlay colors update
- Arrow navigation: :test uses arrow keys, progress counter updates, element-detail panel changes
- Export: :test clicks export-btn, JSON download triggers
- State persistence: :test classifies elements, refreshes page, classifications restored from localStorage

The recursive setup: SL drives a browser pointed at the TL UI, the TL UI calls SL's sieve server to sieve a target app (demo-app at localhost:3000). Three layers deep — SL testing the tool that builds SL glossaries.

Depends on: dogfood glossary bead (testids + intent region must exist first). Also needs TL UI served via HTTP and demo-app running.
close_reason: 6 Gherkin feature files using ToddlerLoop.* intent references: navigate, sieve, classify, element navigation, export, metadata. Intent bindings resolve to CSS data-testid selectors. Created leftglove-w2b to add :testid as first-class SL selector.

title: Bridge — SL config loading and browser provisioning
description: bridge/shiftlefter.clj — accept path to project under test, load SL config via config/load-config-safe, load step definitions. bridge/browser.clj — provision browser instance via SL adapter registry. All paths absolute (no SL_USER_CWD needed). Verify: from REPL, init bridge with a shiftlefter.edn path, open a browser, navigate to a URL.
design: Files: leftglove/bridge/shiftlefter.clj (init, config, steps), leftglove/bridge/browser.clj (provision, navigate, execute-script wrapper). Consumes: SL runner.config, stepengine.registry, adapters.registry. Produces: initialized bridge state that sieve and tools use.
acceptance_criteria: From REPL: (bridge/init! {:config-path "/abs/path/to/shiftlefter.edn"}) succeeds. (bridge/browser! :web) provisions a browser. (bridge/navigate! browser "https://example.com") loads the page. Step registry is populated after init.
notes: STALE: Bridge no longer provisions browsers — SL handles that behind HTTP. Bridge is now: read glossary EDN files + call SL HTTP endpoints. Much smaller scope.
close_reason: Stale — bridge is now HTTP calls to SL. Absorbed into MCP server task.

title: Toddler loop UI v0 — screenshot + SVG overlay + keyboard classification
description: Build the toddler loop UI as a single HTML/JS file at leftglove/toddler/index.html. No build step, no npm, no framework. Open in browser, works immediately. DATA SOURCE: Fetches from SL's sieve HTTP endpoint (localhost:3333). - POST /sieve → JSON inventory (elements with categories, labels, locators, rects, regions) - GET /screenshot → PNG of current page - POST /navigate → tell SL to go to a URL - GET /status → current browser state LAYOUT: Full-page screenshot with SVG overlays on top, fixed bottom panel (200-250px) with element info and controls. NOT a modal, NOT a second window. See leftglove/notes/toddler-loop.md § UI Layout for the full design. PASS 1 — CLASSIFY (breadth-first, rapid-fire): - Show screenshot with current element highlighted (cyan border) - Already-classified elements get colored borders (green=clickable, blue=typable, yellow=readable, gray=chrome, purple=custom) - Bottom panel left: element info (tag, type, sieve label, region, locators) - Bottom panel right: category buttons with keyboard shortcuts c=clickable, t=typable, r=readable, x=chrome, u=custom, /=split, .=skip - One keystroke advances to next element - Progress counter: #17/47 - Arrow keys to go back/forward PASS 2 — LABEL (not needed for v0, but leave room in the data model): - Will add name input field, accept/skip/merge buttons later CONTROLS: - 'Sieve' button → POST /sieve, GET /screenshot, re-render - 'Navigate' button or URL bar → POST /navigate with URL, then auto-sieve - Classification summary shown after all elements classified SVG OVERLAY: - Screenshot displayed as at full page dimensions, scrollable - SVG layer on top with same dimensions - Rects drawn from element.rect (absolute page coordinates) - Current element: thick cyan border, slight background tint - Classified elements: colored borders by category - Unclassified: thin dashed gray border - Chrome elements: very subtle, almost invisible border STATE: - Keep classification state in a JS object: {elementIndex: 0, classifications: {}, pass: 1} - Save/load state to localStorage so you don't lose progress on refresh - Export button: dump classifications as JSON IMPORTANT: The sieve returns elements with rects in absolute page coordinates. The SVG viewBox must match the full-page screenshot dimensions. The screenshot and SVG must scroll together.
close_reason: Toddler loop UI v0 built — single HTML file with screenshot+SVG overlay, keyboard classification (c/t/r/x/u), localStorage persistence, export

title: MCP protocol layer and dummy tool

acceptance_criteria: MCP client (e.g. Claude Desktop or mcp-cli) connects via stdio. initialize handshake completes with server capabilities. Client calls echo tool, gets response. Invalid JSON-RPC returns proper error codes.
notes: MERGE WITH leftglove-05g. With @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, the scaffold IS the protocol layer — the SDK handles JSON-RPC, lifecycle, capability negotiation. These are one task. Do 05g first (project setup), then 637 is just registering the echo tool and verifying handshake.

title: Raw SVOI execution path in SL

title: Autonomous toddler exploration — multi-page crawl with state isolation
description: The sieve is fast enough (~50ms for 409 elements on Slashdot) that letting toddlers wander between pages is achievable. Design: toddler follows links, sieves each page, builds cross-page inventory. Needs constraints to prevent parallel toddlers from corrupting each other's state — e.g., unauthenticated-only mode, separate browser contexts, or turn-based exploration. The sorting/classification UI also needs significant work to handle this volume (409 elements per page × N pages). Needs grooming: state isolation model, UI for multi-page inventory, crawl limits, duplicate detection across pages.

Controllable via env var (e.g. SIEVE=0 to skip) for sessions that don't need a browser. user.clj already does spec instrumentation on startup — add sieve-server/start! after that. Store the env atom in a def so it's accessible from the REPL (e.g. (def sieve-env ...)). Print the sieve server URL in the startup banner. SL CODEBASE WORK — modify dev/user.clj.

Currently localStorage-only — state dies with the browser tab, can't be shared, can't survive across machines. Naming convention for multiple sieve sessions (URL-based? timestamp? both?). Multiple people working on same app need to share state. The export JSON is close to the intermediate format already. For the demo, load-pre-labeled (leftglove-4j8) is sufficient. For the product, auto-save is table stakes. The intermediate format is working state / scratch paper — the glossary in git is the source of truth, but the working state needs to persist between sessions.

title: SL: Glossary HTTP endpoints on sieve server
description: Add two endpoints to SL's sieve HTTP server (server.clj). POST /glossary/intents — accepts JSON intent data, reads existing EDN file if present, merges new elements (new wins on collision, never removes), writes back. GET /glossary — returns current glossary state as JSON (all loaded subjects, verbs, intents). Merge semantics: (update existing :elements merge new-elements). If no intent file exists, create it. Paths derived from shiftlefter.edn :glossaries config. SL CODEBASE WORK — modify shiftlefter/sieve/server.clj and add glossary write helpers.

title: TL UI: sieve diff display
description: Sieve twice, show what appeared/disappeared/changed. New elements get distinct highlight, removed shown as phantoms. For demo Act 3: add checkbox, re-sieve, diff shows +1 element.

title: Sieve limitation: lazy-loaded / below-fold content invisible
description:
Slashdot (and many modern sites) doesn't render content below the fold until scroll. The sieve only sees what's in the DOM, so it returns a partial inventory. This affects any site using Intersection Observer, virtualized lists, infinite scroll, or scroll-triggered rendering.

Slashdot is a good known-broken test case — it's a content-heavy site where we can verify expected elements are missing.

Potential approaches (none implemented, just noting):
- Scroll to bottom before sieving (brute force, breaks infinite scroll)
- Multiple sieve passes at different scroll positions, merge inventories
- Detect lazy-load sentinel elements and trigger them
- Report "partial inventory" with scroll-depth metadata

Ties into collection detection (leftglove-sls) — repeated story elements are the ones being lazy-loaded.

Discovered while dogfooding the sieve against real sites. Not blocking, but important for the "sieve any site" story and for cohort beta testing (other teams' apps may lazy-load too).

title: Sieve metadata expansion — cookies, storage keys, tab count
description:
Expand the sieve return value to include ambient browser state alongside the DOM inventory. This is SL-side sieve work — both sieve.js (in-page) and the sieve server endpoint.

## What to capture

**From sieve.js (in-page JavaScript):**
- localStorage keys: Object.keys(localStorage)
- sessionStorage keys: Object.keys(sessionStorage)

**From the sieve server (Etaoin/WebDriver layer, added to POST /sieve response):**
- All cookie keys for the current domain, including HttpOnly (via Etaoin get-cookies). Keys only — no values, no expiry, no path. Cookie values could contain tokens/PII.
- Tab/window count (via Etaoin window-handles). Did the last action open a new tab?

## Return shape addition

The POST /sieve response currently returns element inventory. Add a top-level `meta` field:

```json
{
  "elements": [...],
  "meta": {
    "cookies": ["session_id", "csrf_token", "_ga"],
    "storage": {
      "localStorage": ["cart", "user_prefs", "theme"],
      "sessionStorage": ["auth_token"]
    },
    "tabs": 1
  }
}
```

## Why this matters

1. **Exit gate detection for role-gated crawling:** The toddler needs to detect when an action caused logout. Pattern: snapshot cookie keys before action, re-sieve after, diff. If session cookie disappeared → role exit. This enables the verify-check model: {:verify {:cookie "session_id"}}.

2. **Side-effect detection:** Some actions have invisible side-effects — a click sets a cookie or writes to localStorage without changing the DOM. Without ambient state in the sieve, these are undetectable.

3. **Clean session baseline:** We start with empty sessions. Any cookies/storage that appear were SET by the app. Free metadata for understanding app behavior.

4. **State persistence classification:** localStorage survives refresh, sessionStorage does not. Useful for understanding what the app considers persistent vs ephemeral.

## Implementation notes

- sieve.js: add a `collectMeta()` function that returns {localStorage: [...], sessionStorage: [...]}. Call it alongside the element inventory. Wrap in try/catch — some iframes or cross-origin contexts may deny access.
- sieve server (server.clj): after running sieve.js via evaluate, also call Etaoin's (get-cookies driver) and (get-window-handles driver). Merge into the response under :meta.
- The element inventory is unchanged — this is purely additive.
- Cookie keys from both sources (document.cookie via sieve.js AND Etaoin get-cookies) should be merged and deduplicated. Etaoin's list is authoritative (includes HttpOnly) but the sieve.js list catches cookies the WebDriver API might miss in edge cases.
close_reason: Added storage keys (sieve.js), cookie keys and tab count (Etaoin) to sieve response. Raw Etaoin calls — protocol port tracked as leftglove-xl7.

title: Dogfood test environment script
description:
Shell script (bin/dogfood-test or similar) that stands up the services needed for dogfood testing and runs SL against the TL UI.

Services needed:
- SL sieve server (configurable port, default 3333, test: 3334)
- Demo app (configurable port, default 3000, test: 3001)
- TL UI served via HTTP (configurable port, default 8080, test: 8081)

Script should: start services on test ports, wait for health checks (GET /status, HTTP 200, etc.), run sl run against the dogfood feature files, tear down on exit (trap cleanup).

Prerequisite: TL UI API URL must be configurable (currently hardcoded to localhost:3333). Add query param support (e.g. ?api=http://localhost:3334) or similar mechanism so the test browser sees the test sieve port.

Run-level config, not test-level. Individual Gherkin scenarios don't know about ports or services. The script (or shiftlefter.edn :environment section) handles that.
close_reason: Already implemented in f7e9881. bin/dogfood-test script, configurable API URL via ?api= query param, notes on sieve perception layer.

title: MCP vocabulary projection — glossary to MCP tools
description: Read SL glossary files (subjects.edn, verbs/web.edn, glossary/intents/*.edn). Parse EDN. Project as dynamic MCP tool schemas. Agent sees Login.email, Login.password, Login.submit as references. Builds on MCP scaffold.

title: Dogfood: TL UI testids + ShiftLefter glossary
description:
Add data-testid attributes to TL UI elements that lack stable selectors (classification buttons, arrow nav buttons, panel sections). Existing IDs are good: url-input, btn-navigate, btn-sieve, btn-export, status-indicator, screenshot-img, overlay-svg, progress, classified-count.

Write a ShiftLefter project config for the TL UI as the app under test. Includes:

subjects.edn:
  :test — singleton, system verification actor
  :developer — instances [:alice], builds features and writes code
  :qa — instances [:travis], reviews classifications and glossary
  :brownfield — instances [:bob], onboards an existing application

ToddlerLoop intent region with element bindings for: url-input, navigate-btn, sieve-btn, export-btn, status-indicator, screenshot-img, overlay-svg, classification buttons (click/type/read/chrx/custom/split/skip), arrow-prev, arrow-next, progress, classified-count, element-detail panel, toast.

Use SL default web verbs (click, fill, see, navigate). Location TBD — probably leftglove/toddler/sl-tests/ or similar.
close_reason: data-testid on all TL UI elements (9 new + 12 mirrored from existing IDs). SL project config with subjects, verbs, and intent region. Canonical locator convention documented in ARCHITECTURE.md invariants.

title: Agent context generation — dynamic project prompt + domain guides
description:
Supersedes WI-GP.005 (sl agent-prompt). Three overlapping needs:

1. DYNAMIC PROJECT CONTEXT (from GP.005): CLI command or HTTP endpoint that reads actual project state — shiftlefter.edn, registered step patterns, glossary entries (subjects, verbs, intents) — and outputs a project-specific prompt fragment. An agent gets not just 'how SL works' but 'what THIS project's vocabulary and step library contains.'

2. DOMAIN VOCABULARY GUIDE: Teach an agent to read glossaries and understand the allowed behaviors and intents of the system. 'Here are the subjects, here are the verbs per interface, here are the intents and their elements.' Oriented toward an agent that needs to understand what the app can do, not write Clojure.

3. TEST EXECUTION GUIDE: Teach an agent to run sl run, interpret exit codes (0=pass, 1=fail, 2=planning error), read failure output, and act on it. Oriented toward QA/verification agents.

Existing assets: docs/AGENT.md (400 lines, covers SL mechanics for developer agents — defstep, CLI, SVO model, project structure). GET /glossary endpoint on sieve server (returns full glossary state as JSON). leftglove-h34 (MCP vocabulary projection) is a related downstream consumer.

Needs grooming to decide: static docs vs dynamic generation, where they live (SL vs demo project vs LG), which pieces are SL code changes vs guide documents.

title: TL UI: verify sieve metadata (cookies, storage, tabs)
description:
The sieve POST /sieve response now includes ambient browser state from leftglove-def:

- storage.localStorage: [] (keys from sieve.js, in-page)
- storage.sessionStorage: [] (keys from sieve.js, in-page)
- cookies: [] (keys from Etaoin get-cookies, includes HttpOnly)
- tabs: N (from Etaoin get-window-handles)

The demo app should exercise this:
1. Login page sets a session cookie — verify it appears in cookies after sieve
2. Add a page that writes to localStorage (e.g., theme preference) — verify key appears in storage.localStorage
3. Optionally: a link that opens target=_blank — verify tabs increments
4. TL UI should display ambient state somewhere (status bar? metadata panel?) so the human can see cookies/storage at a glance

This is about verifying the SL-side sieve metadata works end-to-end through the TL UI, not about building new SL features.
close_reason: Metadata strip in TL UI displays cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, and tab count from sieve response. Demo app dashboard writes localStorage keys and has target=_blank link to exercise all three metadata types.

title: Observation loop — declared intent click
description: Human clicks element in SVG overlay → TL UI tells SL to execute that click on the live DOM (POST /click or similar SL endpoint) → wait for quiescence → re-sieve → element identity matching → diff display. This is the core exploration mechanism: 'Daddy tells the toddler to click #17.' The human directs, the toddler executes, the system records intent + outcome. Also supports programmatic exploration: 'what happens if you click #17?' After click: new observation, diff from previous, names carried forward. May need new SL endpoint for 'click element by locator' or can compose from existing sieve server capabilities.

title: Element identity across observations
description: When TL UI re-sieves (after code change, click, or navigation), match elements between old and new inventories to carry forward classifications and names. Matching tiers: (1) stable locators (id, testid, name) → automatic carry-forward, (2) composite key (region + tag + label) → high confidence, (3) unmatched → flag as new or removed. Persistent elements (nav, footer) carry names forward automatically. New elements enter the toddler loop. The 80/20 applies: composite key handles most cases, human reviews the rest. Without this, every re-sieve is a blank slate and diff display can only show counts, not 'Login.remember-me appeared.'

title: TL UI: timing display in status bar
description: Show sieve performance: Sieved 409 elements in 127ms. Measure round-trip for POST /sieve. Useful for demo and debugging.

title: TL UI: Pass 2 naming mode
description: Add Pass 2 mode to toddler loop UI. Mode switch after Pass 1 complete or manual toggle. Bottom panel right side gets: name input field with smart pre-fill (testid > id > name > label-transform), intent region field (auto-derived from sieve region, editable, can create new), locator display (show all available, highlight selected best). Arrow keys still navigate. Named elements show names on SVG overlay. Data model addition: names{} and intentRegions{} alongside classifications{}. Persists to localStorage.

title: Service dependencies as fixture contracts (design only)
description:
Design note for future: service availability is a kind of fixture precondition, analogous to data fixtures. 'Sieve server must be running on port X' is structurally similar to 'user alice must exist with credentials Y.' Both are things that must be true before tests execute.

SL's fixture macro system could eventually unify these:
- Data fixtures: per-test/suite, managed by test framework (existing)
- Service fixtures: per-environment, managed by infrastructure (new)

A fixture contract that says 'ensure sieve server is running' follows the same enter/verify/exit pattern as role gate pairs:
- Enter: start the service
- Verify: health check passes
- Exit: stop the service

This is NOT a build-it-now task. It's a design breadcrumb for when SL's fixture/contract system matures. The immediate need (dogfood test env) is solved by a shell script. This bead captures the insight that the pattern generalizes.

Connects to: fixture contracts (notes/fixture-contracts.md), gate pairs from feature-vision, EP-035 (intent region completion).

title: Collection detection — repeated elements with shared structure
description: The sieve must detect DOM siblings that share a structural template — e.g., 30 story links on Hacker News, 50 Q&A pairs on a corporate FAQ, card grids on any aggregator (Slashdot, Reddit, product listings). Without this, the toddler loop asks 'what is this?' 30 times for the same kind of element. The sieve should detect structural repetition (N children of a container with the same tag/class/nesting pattern), report it as a collection with a template and count, and the toddler loop should present the collection as one group for single-classification. Example: 'I found 30 items that look like [link + domain + score]. What are these?' → Human says 'StoryList.story' → all 30 classified at once. Demo app may include collections depending on how fast we go.
notes: 2026-04-07: Deferring. Need real TL loop usage data before designing collection detection. Template fingerprinting (tag-only? tag+class? nesting depth? threshold count?) is empirical — every real page surprises differently. Wait until Stephen is classifying real pages and hitting the repeated-element pain, then we'll know what grouping should look like.

title: Demo app — standalone login page for demo acts
description: Standalone HTML page served locally. Login form with email, password, submit, forgot password link. Known element inventory (~7-10 elements). Supports the add Remember Me checkbox code change (3-line diff). Must be runnable by sl run with Gherkin tests.
close_reason: Standalone Express demo app with login page, dashboard, session handling, 10 sieveable elements

title: diff tool — structural delta between observations
description: Compute and present structural differences between two sieve outputs. What elements appeared, disappeared, changed state.
notes: Promoted to demo scope. TL UI implementation is leftglove-cuo. This issue can be closed in favor of that one, or kept if we also want a standalone diff tool/MCP tool later.
close_reason: Absorbed into leftglove-cuo (TL UI sieve diff display). If standalone diff MCP tool needed later, create new bead.

title: Add :testid as first-class selector type
description: Add :testid to valid-selector-types in browser/locators.clj. When resolving, expand {:testid "foo"} → {:css "[data-testid='foo']"}. This makes data-testid a first-class locator in SL, matching the canonical locator convention established in LG. Currently intent bindings must use {:css "[data-testid='btn-sieve']"} — with this, they can use {:testid "btn-sieve"}. Change is in locators.clj resolve-map (expand before passing to Etaoin) and add :testid to valid-selector-types. Once done, update LG's toddler-loop.edn intent bindings to use the cleaner format.
close_reason: Added :testid as first-class selector type, expands to CSS [data-testid='...']

title: Port sieve server from raw Etaoin to IBrowser protocol
description:
The sieve server (sieve/server.clj) calls Etaoin directly for everything: js-execute (sieve.js injection), go (navigation), get-url, get-title, get-cookies, get-window-handles, screenshot. It should use IBrowser instead, so Playwright works for free.

Scope:
1. Add to IBrowser protocol: js-execute, get-cookies (keys only), get-window-count, screenshot (bytes)
2. Implement in both adapters (etaoin.clj, playwright.clj)
3. Refactor sieve/server.clj: accept IBrowser instead of raw Etaoin driver
4. start! provisions browser via adapter registry instead of raw eta/chrome

This is architecturally correct but not urgent — the sieve server works fine with Etaoin for dev use. Priority rises if we want to demo with Playwright or if the adapter gap becomes confusing.

Context: leftglove-def (sieve metadata expansion) added get-cookies and get-window-handles as raw Etaoin calls, explicitly noting this shortcut. This bead cleans that up along with all the other raw calls.
notes: 2026-04-07: Adapter selection concern — currently start! hardcodes (eta/chrome). After port, read adapter type from shiftlefter.edn :interfaces :web :adapter (:etaoin or :playwright). Override to headed mode since sieve needs a visible browser (project config may say headless for CI). start! could take {:adapter :from-config :headed true}. Separate env var (SIEVE_ADAPTER) is over-engineering until someone asks. LG (o3e etc) won't notice when this lands — POST /sieve response shape is identical, purely internal refactor.
close_reason: Sieve server ported to IBrowser protocol. Added js-execute, screenshot-bytes, get-cookies, get-window-count to protocol. Implemented in both Etaoin and Playwright adapters. start! uses adapter registry.

title: Glossary generation — toddler labels to SL glossary EDN
description: Classification JSON from toddler UI to SL-compatible glossary EDN files. Could be script, TL UI feature, or MCP server piece. For demo may hand-write glossary, but pipeline should exist.
close_reason: Absorbed into leftglove-07k (TL UI Write Glossary integration). SL glossary HTTP endpoints (leftglove-cms) already built — the real task is the TL UI button that calls them.

title: Verify sl run + strict mode against demo app
description: Confirm sl run executes Gherkin against demo app and passes. Strict mode rejects unknown glossary refs. After deleting Remember Me checkbox, sl run fails clearly. SL work tracked here because it blocks demo.
close_reason: Premature — demo app still being built in LG. Will open appropriate verification bead once demo stabilizes.
